summary: Full backend + frontend E2E testing of Pipeline feature. All 11 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend flows verified: pipeline tab, create, run now (stats update), pause/resume, edit, delete, specific times schedule, require approval toggle.

issues: Form pre-selects first 2 client platforms (clientPlatforms.slice(0,2)) by default. If user clicks only 'instagram', they may end up with 3 platforms selected (linkedin + twitter + instagram) without realizing it. Minor UX issue - could confuse users who expect a blank slate.

action_items: Minor: Pipeline form pre-selects first 2 client platforms by default. Consider showing all unselected so user explicitly picks platforms.
critical_code_review_comments: execute_pipeline inserts post with {**post} causing _id duplication issue (post dict is used directly then passed to insert_one with spread - OK since no _id key in post dict)
calculate_next_run: specific_times doesn't handle empty list edge case (falls through without returning anything when times=[])

PipelineUpdate uses {k: v for k, v in ... if v is not None} - this means you can't set boolean fields to False or lists to [] via update (e.g., disabling require_approval won't work). Use model_dump(exclude_unset=True) instead.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created and deleted TEST_ pipelines. Daily LinkedIn Carousel pipeline now has Total Runs=2.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pipeline feature fully working. Backend: GET/POST /api/clients/{id}/pipelines, PUT/DELETE/pause/resume/run all passing. Frontend: All flows tested - create, run now (stats update to show total_runs and last_run), pause/resume toggle, edit with pre-fill, delete with confirm dialog, specific_times schedule with time tags, require approval toggle. MOCKED: publisher.py mock_publish for social publishing. Pipeline content generation uses real Claude AI.
mocked_apis: publisher.py mock_publish - social media publishing is MOCKED
